rationale: The citing paper’s core contribution is an “incentive-aligned” preference-elicitation method using BDM-style willingness-to-pay and out-of-sample validation, which closely overlaps with the cited paper’s core contribution of incentive-aligned conjoint/valuation with real economic consequences and improved predictive accuracy. Given this conceptual and methodological alignment (not just topical similarity), the citation is plausibly foundational/mechanism-related and should be kept for full relation judgment.
